start.title: No Reservations: The Afterlife Tour
start.text: Chef Anthony appears in the quiet glow of the city night. With me, every meal tells a story. Let's travel the world, taste incredible food, and discover the cultures behind every table. Where would you like to go first?

choices.text: Istanbul, Turkey
Tokyo, Japan
New Orleans, USA
Mexico City, Mexico

istanbul.title: Istanbul, Turkey
istanbul.text: Lanterns glow near the Bosphorus as Chef Anthony leads you through the city's ancient streets. "A city split between continents learns how to hold contradictions," he says.

choices.text: Visit the spice market
Eat by the water
Sit down for tea and conversation

istanbul_spice.title: The Spice Market
istanbul_spice.text: The air blooms with saffron, sumac, and tea. "A city is a recipe," Chef Anthony says. "Everything that passed through leaves flavor behind."

istanbul_water.title: By the Water
istanbul_water.text: You eat grilled fish as ferries cross the dark water. "Some places live between worlds," he says. "So do people."

allergy_comments.Seafood: Anthony glances at you. "The fish here is incredible — but we'll make sure the kitchen knows."

istanbul_tea.title: Tea by the Window
istanbul_tea.text: You sit in a quiet café while tea glasses glow amber under the light. Anthony watches the room. "The best places never rush you. They let the city come to you."

choices.text: Ask him what makes a meal unforgettable
Stay silent and listen to the room

istanbul_meal.title: A Table Worth Remembering
istanbul_meal.text: "It has to tell the truth," Anthony says. "About the place. About the people. About whoever cooked it." Outside, the call to prayer rolls through the city like another heartbeat.

istanbul_listen.title: The City Speaks for Itself
istanbul_listen.text: Spoons tap glass. Chairs scrape softly. Someone laughs near the counter. For a while, nobody says anything. Somehow it feels like the right ending.

tokyo.title: Tokyo, Japan
tokyo.text: Neon lights reflect off rain-slick streets as Chef Anthony leads you deeper into the city. "Tokyo rewards attention," he says. "Blink and you'll miss the whole point."

choices.text: Follow the smoke to a yakitori stall
Try monjayaki on a hot griddle
Follow him into a hidden sushi bar

tokyo_yakitori.title: Smoke and Small Stools
tokyo_yakitori.text: Smoke curls above tiny stools outside a counter no wider than a hallway. Chef Anthony says, "Respect the small places. They often hold the biggest truths."

tokyo_monjayaki.title: The Griddle
tokyo_monjayaki.text: You scrape crispy edges from the hot griddle while the batter sizzles and spreads. Chef Anthony grins. "Not every masterpiece looks pretty at first."

allergy_comments.Dairy: Anthony watches the batter spread across the griddle. "There's butter in there. I should have mentioned that earlier."
allergy_comments.Gluten: Anthony winces. "Wheat batter on a hot griddle. This one's not for you — but watch it cook. That's still worth something."

tokyo_sushi.title: Twelve Seats, No Menu
tokyo_sushi.text: The sushi bar is quiet except for the sound of knives and soft greetings. You sit down and let the chef decide your meal. Anthony smiles. "Trust is part of the experience."

allergy_comments.Seafood: Anthony raises an eyebrow. "A seafood-free omakase. Unusual. Let's see what the chef does with it."

choices.text: Trust the chef completely
Ask too many questions about every bite

tokyo_trust.title: The Best Seat in the City
tokyo_trust.text: Piece after piece arrives with perfect timing. No music, no spectacle, no wasted motion. Anthony looks almost peaceful. "The world gets loud," he says. "Skill like this doesn't have to."

allergy_comments.Seafood: Anthony leans over quietly. "I let them know. They adjusted. That's what a great kitchen does."

tokyo_questions.title: You Broke the Spell
tokyo_questions.text: The chef stays polite, but the room changes. Anthony gives you a look that says you talked your way out of something beautiful.

allergy_comments.Seafood: Anthony shakes his head. "Between the allergy questions and the food questions, the chef has had enough of both."

new_orleans.title: New Orleans, USA
new_orleans.text: Warm night air wraps around you as jazz spills through the streets. The smell of spice, butter, and sugar hangs in the air. Chef Anthony smiles. "Some cities survive by becoming unforgettable. New Orleans is one of them."

choices.text: Follow the scent of gumbo
Follow the trumpet into a jazz club
Ask why this city matters so much

nola_gumbo.title: The Gumbo Pot
nola_gumbo.text: A woman stirs a massive pot with practiced patience. Chef Anthony leans close. "Good food takes time. So do people worth knowing."

allergy_comments.Seafood: Anthony pauses. "Traditional gumbo has shrimp. She's making you a version without. Don't take that lightly."
allergy_comments.Gluten: Anthony watches the pot. "The roux is the soul of gumbo — and unfortunately, that's flour. She's working around it for you."

nola_jazz.title: After Midnight
nola_jazz.text: Inside a crowded club, strangers dance like old friends. Chef Anthony laughs. "Some people wait for permission to feel alive. Never do that."

nola_talk.title: Street Corner Wisdom
nola_talk.text: He watches the street quietly. "Because this place knows grief," he says. "And it still chose music."

mexico_city.title: Mexico City, Mexico
mexico_city.text: The city hums around you as tacos sizzle on the grill and Chef Anthony smiles. "A city this alive doesn't introduce itself slowly," he says.

choices.text: Try tacos from a crowded stand
Visit the market for mole
Sit down for late-night churros and chocolate

mexico_tacos.title: Standing Room Only
mexico_tacos.text: You eat standing on the sidewalk, grease on your hands and no regrets. Chef Anthony says, "Never confuse simple with ordinary."

allergy_comments.Gluten: Anthony squints. "Corn tortillas. You're fine. If they'd handed you flour, I would have said something."

mexico_mole.title: The Mole
mexico_mole.text: The mole is deep, smoky, sweet, and bitter all at once. "Some flavors take generations to explain," he says. You don't try to explain it. You just finish the plate.

allergy_comments.Nuts: Anthony tilts his head. "Some moles lean on almonds or peanuts. This one doesn't. You got lucky."

mexico_churros.title: Sugar at Midnight
mexico_churros.text: The chocolate is thick, the churros are still hot, and the whole table goes quiet for a second. Anthony nods. "Dessert isn't an afterthought in a city that knows how to live."

allergy_comments.Dairy: Anthony winces slightly. "The chocolate dipping sauce. We'll get you something on the side."
allergy_comments.Gluten: Anthony gives you a look. "Churros. You know what churros are made of. We'll find you something else on this table."

choices.text: Ask him what travel is really for
Order one more plate even though you're full

mexico_travel.title: A Better Kind of Hunger
mexico_travel.text: "Not to collect places," Anthony says. "To let them change you a little." The city keeps moving around you while the thought stays still.

mexico_full.title: One Plate Too Many
mexico_full.text: The second plate seemed like a good idea five minutes ago. Now even Anthony looks concerned. "A good traveler knows when the meal is over," he says.
